top_categories.workout_context: 502 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 502 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
63,173ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Haute Route Alps' alpine mountain passes — HC climbs over 7 days forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 502 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Haute Route Alps' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
502 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
At Haute Route Alps, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
63,173ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
126ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Haute Route Alps, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 63,173ft of climbing on alpine mountain passes — HC climbs over 7 days does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on alpine mountain passes — HC climbs over 7 days. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: August in Nice → Thonon-les-Bains (2026 route) — Late August in the French Alps: valley temperatures 20-30°C, summit temperatures 5-15°C at 2,000-2,700m. Temperature drops ~6.5°C per 1,000m. Over 7 days, encountering 1-2 rainy stages is likely. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Haute Route Alps — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day. Race-day realities: 25-30°C temperature range daily — valley heat to summit cold.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 502 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 502 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Haute Route Alps with 63,173ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. 25-30°C temperature range daily — valley heat to summit cold.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Haute Route Alps demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride alpine mountain passes — hc climbs over 7 days at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 502 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Endurance, and VO2max to prepare you for 502 miles of alpine mountain passes — hc climbs over 7 days in Nice → Thonon-les-Bains (2026 route).
